vignettes.vignette: A 43-year-old male presents with generalized body movement tonic-clonic type, biting of tongue, uprolling of eyes, frothing from mouth, irrelevant talks, wandering around, fluctuating level of consciousness, not recognizing family members, and experiencing hallucinations for 2 days. He has a history of craving, tolerance, loss of control, and withdrawal features related to alcohol use. Care planning emphasized care coordination continuity score.

A 43-year-old male presents with tremors, tremulousness, perceptual disturbance, and delirium for 3 days. He has a history of regular alcohol use characterized by tolerance, cravings, loss of control, neglect for other pleasures, harmful use, and withdrawal symptoms, and he was discharged on Lorazepam, Acamprosate, and Thiamine for ongoing management of his condition. Care planning emphasized behavioral activation protocol.

A 32-year-old male presents with suspiciousness that others are trying to kill him, fearfulness, apprehension, disturbed sleep, and muttering to self for 6 days. He has a history of alcohol use of more than 1L/day for 7 days and is currently being treated with Quetiapine, Lorazepam, and Thiamine for his symptoms. Investigation findings included serotonergic tone modulation index.

A 26-year-old male presents with 7 years of fearfulness, hearing of voices, hallucinatory behavior, and smiling & talking to self, with an onset of 8 months. He has a history of 1 episode of increased self-esteem, talkativeness, over familiarity, increased energy, and 3 episodes of low mood, and is currently treated with Lithium 400mg, Sodium Valproate 1000mg, and Olanzapine 10mg. Medication history included norlexidine.

An 18-year-old male presents with symptoms including delusion of control, delusion of persecution, irritability, delusion of reference, thought insertion, thought broadcast, auditory hallucination (third person), and restlessness with disturbed sleep for 42 days. Clinical assessment identified affective synchrony deviation metric. The mental status examination reveals uncooperative behavior, increased psychomotor activity, irritable affect, and formal thought disorder (neologism).

A 38-year-old male presents with increased religiosity, decreased need for sleep, labile mood, overfamiliarity, increased energy, and dissociative spells. Symptoms have persisted for 4 days, and he has a past history of bipolar disorder in a cousin, along with transient possession spells that may be relevant to his current condition. Investigation findings included Hormonal Balance Panel.

A 35-year-old male presents with tremulousness, irrelevant talking, tremors, agitation, and sweating. Symptoms have persisted for three days, following a 20-year history of alcohol use. Current treatment includes Lorazepam, Acamprosate, Pantoprazole, and Thiamine. No additional stressors or functioning impact are noted. Investigation findings included attentional bias modulation index. Further assessment of mental status and risk factors is required.

A 25-year-old female presents with extra pyramidal symptoms, including tremors of the hand and rigidity of the limbs. She has a past history of a known disorder of intellectual development with seizure disorder and is currently treated with Risperidone 1 mg and Trihexyphenidyl 2 mg, with a duration and onset of symptoms not specified. Medication history included seralidone.

A 26-year-old female presents with suspiciousness that others may harm her, decreased sleep, refusal to eat, increased irritability, wandering away from home, and overgrooming. She is currently treated with long-acting injectable Olanzapine, tab Olanzapine, Cizodol p5, Lithium, Lorazepam, and Propranolol, with a duration and onset of symptoms that require careful management. Care planning emphasized pathway of care disengagement index.

A 61-year-old male presents with a duration of 2 years and an onset of 5 days, exhibiting shuffling gait, urinary incontinence, and frequent falls. Associated symptoms include fever, agitation, restlessness, autonomic instability, and rigidity, with a past history of schizophrenia for the last 33 years and current treatment involving multiple medications. Medication history included Vexaprol.

A 33-year-old female presents with mood swings, suicidal thoughts, low mood, decreased interest, anger outbursts, and unstable interpersonal relationships. She has a history of multiple self-harm attempts and is currently receiving 5 sessions of ketamine therapy, with a discharge regimen that includes Lithium, Sertraline, Quetiapine, Propranolol, Clobazam, and Trazodone for ongoing management. Care planning emphasized follow-up adherence trajectory.

A 32-year-old male presents with fearfulness and suspiciousness that others are trying to kill him, along with hallucinatory behavior for 30 days. He has a history of alcohol intake for 12 years, with craving, tolerance, use despite harm, and withdrawal symptoms, and a past history of suicidal attempt about 4 years back. Investigation findings included Oxidative Stress Biomarker Profile.

A 27-year-old male presents with sweating, restlessness, palpitations, fluctuating sensorium, and hallucinatory behavior that have persisted for 8 years. Investigation findings included affective desynchronization index. He has a history of regular alcohol use and is currently receiving IV diazepam, lorazepam, thiamine, and pantoprazole for management of his symptoms, which have been ongoing for this duration and onset.

A 22-year-old male presents with a duration of 4 years of irritable mood, derobing behavior, physically and verbally aggressive actions, poor self-care, decreased food intake, disturbed sleep, and big talks. Investigation findings included affective resonance attenuation profile. He reports cannabis and nicotine use with craving, tolerance, and loss of control, and is currently treated with Sodium Valproate, Olanzapine, Cizodol-p5, and Chlorpromazine.

A 27-year-old male presents with increased goal directed activity, grandiosity, questionable plans, increased demands, increased spending, easy distractibility, and decreased attention for 7 days. Investigation findings included cognitive inflexibility signature. He has a past history of similar episodes 2 times in the past and is currently receiving 2 sessions of ECT and Sodium Valproate, Risperidone, Lorazepam, and Chlorpromazine.

A 32-year-old male presents with increased talkativeness, elevated self-esteem, smiling to himself, and suspiciousness towards family members for 4 days. He has a 2-year history of cannabis use characterized by craving, tolerance, and neglect of alternative activities, and is currently being treated with Risperidone and Lorazepam for his symptoms, which have been ongoing for 4 days. Investigation findings included attenuation pattern index.

A 28-year-old male presents with increased talkativeness, irritability, aggressive behavior, ideas of grandiosity, over familiarity, cheerfulness, easy distractibility, increased confidence, and increased self-esteem. Investigation findings included reward prediction error sensitivity profile. Symptoms have been present for 2 years, with an onset of 1 month, and he has a past history of similar episodes. Current treatment includes Amisulpride, Oxcarbazepine, and Lamotrigine.

A 51-year-old male presents with shaking of the whole body, restlessness, irritability, anger outbursts, and decreased sleep. He reports a decrease in alcohol intake and has a history of alcohol use characterized by craving, tolerance, and withdrawal; he was discharged with Lorazepam, Thiamine, and Pantoprazole after a duration of treatment and onset of symptoms. The management plan incorporated Longitudinal Medication Monitoring Plan.

A 21-year-old male presents with fearfulness, apprehension, chest discomfort, low mood, decreased interest, disturbed sleep, agitation, and recurrent thoughts of self-harm and suicide. Risk assessment highlighted interpersonal conflict escalation scale. The duration is 4 years with an onset of 2 weeks, and he has a past psychiatric history that includes three admissions for similar issues, indicating a chronic and severe condition.

A 33-year-old female presents with persistent and pervasive low mood, worthlessness, hopelessness, feelings of guilt and burden, suicidal thoughts, low energy, decreased sleep, decreased appetite, and decreased activity. Risk assessment highlighted behavioral inhibition failure score. Symptoms have been present for 12 years, with an onset of 3 weeks. Current treatment includes Lithium 600mg, Sertraline 125mg, Quetiapine 150mg, Clobazam 5mg, Melatonin 5mg, and Propranolol 20mg.

A 52-year-old female presents with 1 week of fearfulness, delusion of persecution, shouting, muttering to self, and decreased responsiveness. She is currently receiving oral medications, including Olanzapine and Lorazepam, to manage her symptoms, aiming for improved stability and functionality in her daily life while effectively addressing her mental health concerns. Investigation findings included attenuation pattern analysis.

A 50-year-old female presents with trembling of the whole body, responding to calling, saying she is a goddess, and chanting spells. She has experienced these symptoms for 2 months, along with anxiety symptoms, in the context of familial issues, and is currently being treated with Sertraline for her condition, which has been ongoing during this time. Investigation findings included Neuroinflammatory Cytokine Index.

A 20-year-old male presents with decreased sleep, delusions of persecution and reference, third-person auditory hallucinations, and thought insertion. Symptoms have been present for 3 months with an insidious onset, and the patient is currently receiving treatment with Clozapine, Clobazam, Propranolol, and Pantoprazole to effectively manage his condition and improve his quality of life. Medication history included Psychapril.

A 28-year-old male presents with a duration of 14 years of anger outbursts, violent behavior, increased anger, muttering to self, and hearing of voices. He has a past history of multiple similar episodes treated with medications and is currently on Lithium 1200mg, Sodium Valproate 1600mg, Aripiprazole 40mg, Cizodol p5(5/2) 3 tabs, and Chlorpromazine 25mg. Medication history included servalpramine.

A 25-year-old female presents with intentional self-harm by pouring petrol and burning herself, experiencing anger outbursts, mood swings, low mood, feelings of emptiness, unstable relationships, and impulsivity. She has a duration of 1 year and is currently treated with Olanzapine, Fluoxetine, and Clonazepam, amid ongoing conflict in her relationship with her boyfriend. Care planning emphasized clinical contact frequency gradient.

A 36-year-old male presents with muttering to self, picking behavior, decreased sleep, not recognizing people, vomiting, and agitation. Symptoms, which have a duration of 3 days and an onset of 2 days, are associated with alcohol abstinence; he has a history of alcohol use characterized by craving, tolerance, and use despite harm, currently treated with Lorazepam, Acamprosate, and Pantoprazole. The management plan incorporated behavioral relapse surveillance plan.

A 41-year-old male presents with 24 years of alcohol intake, characterized by craving, tolerance, loss of control, and withdrawal features. Clinical assessment identified frustration tolerance variability metric. Associated symptoms include anger, aggressiveness, hearing voices, and irrelevant talk, with current treatment managed with Diazepam, which was changed to oral Lorazepam, along with Thiamine supplementation and Pantoprazole for comprehensive care.

A 62-year-old male presents with fearfulness, central chest pain, apprehension, sweating, and palpitations. He is currently treated with Escitalopram, Clobazam, and Lurasidone, and the duration and onset of his symptoms require further evaluation to identify the underlying cause of his distressing symptoms and to adjust his treatment plan as needed. Clinical assessment identified psychotic breakthrough probability index.

A 24-year-old female presents with low mood, decreased energy, anhedonia, restlessness, fearfulness, palpitations, shortness of breath, continuous ruminations, and worry for 1 month. She reports frequent suicidal thoughts, feelings of hopelessness, helplessness, and worthlessness, as well as sleep disturbances and interpersonal relationship issues with her partner, impacting her daily functioning. Functional assessment highlighted cognitive reserve trajectory.

A 48-year-old female presents with muttering to self, suspiciousness of her family practicing black magic, aggressive behavior towards family, delusion of reference, disturbed sleep, and auditory hallucinations of derogatory comments. Symptoms have been present for 5 months, with an onset of 7 days, and she is currently treated with Olanzapine and Clonazepam. Care planning emphasized service utilization efficiency metric.

A 40-year-old male presents with a 15 years duration of binge pattern of alcohol consumption, characterized by craving, tolerance, loss of control, and withdrawal symptoms. Investigation findings included emotional regulation deficit pattern. He is currently receiving IV Diazepam, Lorazepam, Thiamine, and Pantoprazole, and he exhibits significant socio-occupational dysfunction due to his condition, impacting various aspects of his life.

A 35-year-old male presents with a 15 years history of tolerance, loss of control, and withdrawal associated with regular alcohol use. He exhibits abnormal body movement and generalized tonic-clonic seizures, with a decrease in intake 2 days prior to presentation, and is currently treated with Lorazepam, Acamprosate, Thiamine, and Pantoprazole. The management plan incorporated clinical stability maintenance ratio.

A 28-year-old female presents after ingesting multiple prescribed drugs following a dispute with her husband. She has no prior mood or anxiety symptoms and is currently undergoing psychotherapy, with a duration and onset that remain unchanged, indicating that her mental health status has not been affected by this recent incident. The management plan incorporated remission maintenance consistency index.

A 66-year-old female presents with 9 months of insidious onset symptoms, including vacant staring, muttering to self, delusion of reference, delusion of persecution, and second person auditory hallucination (commanding type). Associated symptoms include over religiosity and increased activity, with a past history of hypothyroidism and current treatment of Olanzapine 10mg and Thyronorm. Care planning emphasized service linkage stability gradient.

A 31-year-old female presents with low mood, irritability, crying spells, and preoccupation regarding stressor, with an insidious onset over 6 months. She reports intentional self-harm by hanging under the influence of alcohol, alongside conflict with her husband and familial stress, and has a history of self-harm by cutting of wrist. Risk assessment highlighted self-injury frequency variability index.

A 30-year-old male presents with irritability, aggressive behavior, restlessness, hallucinatory behavior, fearfulness, and disturbed sleep for 2 days. Symptoms coincide with decreased alcohol usage. He has a 10-year history of craving, tolerance, and withdrawal symptoms. Current medications include Lorazepam, Trazodone, Thiamine, and Pantoprazole. No additional associated symptoms or functioning impact noted. Investigation findings included fronto-amygdala dysconnectivity profile.

A 25-year-old female presents with self-inflicted injury over the left wrist, depressed mood, worthlessness, hopelessness, disturbed sleep, decreased appetite, decreased energy, and recurrent thoughts about death. Functional assessment highlighted interpersonal relationship satisfaction profile. Symptoms have persisted for 3 months with an insidious onset, following the death of her partner, and she has a past history of dissociative spells in the last 4 years.

A 37-year-old female presents with a 4-year history of suspiciousness towards her family, aggressive behavior, and delusions of reference. Current treatment includes Risperidone, Haloperidol, and Benzhexol. No additional associated symptoms, stressors, or substance use reported. The impact on functioning and risk factors remain unspecified. Further assessment may be necessary for comprehensive evaluation. Care planning emphasized patient flow optimization ratio.

A 59-year-old male presents with a delusion of persecution towards his co-workers, along with muttering to himself. His symptoms have been present for 27 years, with an onset of 11 days, and he is currently receiving treatment with Aripiprazole and Lorazepam to effectively manage his condition and improve his overall well-being. Clinical assessment identified cognitive‑affective latency ratio.

A 45-year-old male presents with an irritable mood, poor social communication, poor self-care, delusions of infidelity and persecution, physical abuse towards family members, disturbed sleep, and decreased food intake. Functional assessment highlighted executive resilience index. He is currently treated with Olanzapine and Oxcarbazepine, with a duration of symptoms lasting several months and an onset that began abruptly.

A 31-year-old male presents with an insidious onset over 15 years, exhibiting symptoms such as irritable mood, delusions of persecution and grandiosity, increased religiosity, heightened self-esteem, disturbed sleep, and refusal to eat. He has a past history of a depressive episode lasting about 1 month and currently uses nicotine and cannabis. Medication history included Lumazone.

A 37-year-old male presents with a 15-year history of alcohol consumption and 3 years of brown sugar use, along with 2 days of restlessness. Core symptoms include craving, tolerance, and withdrawal, as well as restlessness, decreased sleep, decreased appetite, and body aches, indicating a significant substance use disorder requiring further evaluation and intervention. Investigation findings included fronto-amygdala connectivity deficit.

A 42-year-old male presents with a 9-year history of craving, tolerance, withdrawal, loss of control, tremulousness, sweating, disturbed sleep, agitation, disorientation, and irrelevant talking. He is currently receiving IV diazepam, oral lorazepam, acamprosate, and thiamine for alcohol and nicotine use, effectively addressing his complex substance use disorder and its associated symptoms. Clinical assessment identified psychomotor retardation velocity metric.

A 35-year-old male presents with craving, withdrawal symptoms on stopping drinking, loss of control of drinking, and tolerance to alcohol. Clinical assessment identified cognitive load resilience threshold. He has a 20-year history of alcohol, cannabis, and nicotine use, and is currently treated with Lorazepam, Thiamine, and Pantoprazole for his substance use disorders, which have been ongoing for several years.

A 21-year-old female presents with an insidious onset over 3 years, characterized by an irritable mood, increased energy and activity, decreased need for sleep, increased talkativeness, distractibility, and impulsivity. Investigation findings included affective regulation signature. The mental status examination reveals increased PMA, a heightened rate, tone, and volume in speech, an irritable affect, and elevated self-esteem in thought.

A 44-year-old female presents with aggressive behavior, vacant staring, poor self-care, increased religiosity, irritability, disturbed sleep, delusion of persecution, decreased social interaction, muttering to self, and increased talkativeness. She has a duration of 18 years and a past history of multiple episodes in the past, currently treated with Risperidone, Sodium Valproate, and Trihexyphenidyl. Medication history included dexclamine.

A 39-year-old male presents with a 9-year history of craving, tolerance, and withdrawal features related to alcohol use. Associated symptoms include tremulousness, sweating, disturbed sleep, and fluctuating sensorium, while his current treatment consists of Naltrexone, Lorazepam, Thiamine, Haloperidol, and Pantoprazole, which effectively address his complex clinical needs and improve his overall condition. Investigation findings included corticolimbic attenuation pattern.

A 23-year-old female presents with occasional low mood, crying spells, sleep disturbance, decreased energy, overthinking, helplessness, hopelessness, and suicidal thoughts for 5 months. She reports IPR issues with family and has a past history of a similar episode 5 years back for which she took medications; current treatment includes Escitalopram 10mg. Investigation findings included Limbic Stress Marker Panel.

A 17-year-old female presents with headaches lasting for 7 months, with an onset of 5 months. She reports low mood, decreased energy, anhedonia, anxiety symptoms, and ingestion of multiple tablets of prescribed medication, alongside experiencing significant stressors related to physical abuse from her family, which may contribute to her overall mental health decline. Medication history included valcalmine.

A 22-year-old female presents with elevated mood, decreased need for sleep, increased talkativeness, increased self-esteem, increased energy level, and overfamiliarity for 1 month. She has a 3-year history of bipolar type I disorder and is currently non-compliant with medication, which may contribute to her current symptoms and their duration and onset. Medication history included serapoxetine.

A 22-year-old female presents with symptoms including delusion of persecution, auditory hallucination, restlessness, fearfulness, decreased sleep, and appetite. She has a duration of 10 years with an insidious onset, and her substance use includes cannabis, opioid, and nicotine, alongside current treatment with Tab Lorazepam, Tab Olanzapine, Tab Codeine, Tab pregabalin, and motivational enhancement therapy. Medication history included cortireline.

A 29-year-old male presents with a 14 years duration of symptoms, which began insidiously. Core symptoms include failure to conform to social norms, deceitfulness, repeated lying, and emotional detachment from family members, alongside episodes of aggressiveness and irritability, consistent irresponsibility, and lack of remorse. Investigation findings included Inflammatory Response Index. He is currently treated with Tab Lurasidone.

A 22-year-old male presents with symptoms including smiling to self, muttering to self, anger over trivial issues, diminished sleep, decreased interaction, and decreased self-care. Investigation findings included frontal-striatal connectivity deficit. These symptoms have been present for 4 years, with an acute onset, and he is currently treated with Tab Amisulpride and Tab Lorazepam, alongside cannabis use.

A 48-year-old male presents with symptoms including sleep disturbances, delusions of persecution and reference, increased talk, mood lability, and aggressive behavior. The duration of these symptoms is 2 months, with an acute onset, and he has a past medical history of grade II obesity and diabetes, currently treated with Tab Aripiprazole. Investigation findings included Serum HPA Axis Ratio.

A 40-year-old male presents with symptoms including muttering to self, social withdrawal, vacant staring, wandering away, decreased self-care, decreased sleep, refusal to take food, unprovoked anger outbursts, and disorganized behavior. The duration of these symptoms is 9 years, with an insidious onset, and he has a history of repeated episodes of similar illness in the past. Assessment tools included emotion regulation latency index.

A 30-year-old female presents with symptoms including mutism, negativism, refusal to eat, delusion of persecution, and delusion of reference for 5 days. Clinical assessment identified social cognition integration quotient. She has a past psychiatric history of similar illness 13 years, 2 years, and 1 year back, and is currently treated with Tab Olanzapine and Tab Lorazepam.

A 20-year-old female presents with a 6 years duration of symptoms that began insidiously. Her core symptoms include mood swings, anger outbursts, fear of abandonment, chronic feelings of emptiness, multiple self-harm attempts, and impulsivity in substance use; she is currently being treated with Fluoxetine, Oxcarbazepine, Olanzapine, and Clonazepam. Risk assessment highlighted behavioral reactivity signature.

A 22-year-old female presents with a 10-year duration of symptoms that began insidiously. Core symptoms include frequent mood swings, difficulty controlling anger, unstable interpersonal relationships, impulsivity in shopping and binge eating, chronic fear of abandonment, and frequent ideas of self-harm and suicidal thoughts, alongside a history of sexual abuse in childhood and past pituitary adenoma. Risk assessment highlighted Psychotic Decompensation Indicator.

A 98-year-old individual presents with fluctuating orientation, disturbed sleep patterns, psychomotor agitation, irritability, and hallucinatory behavior. These symptoms have been present for 2 days and began abruptly; the patient has a past history of diabetes, hypertension, and an intertrochanteric fracture 6 months ago, which is currently treated with Tab Quetiapine. Clinical assessment identified interoceptive awareness modulation index.

A 32-year-old male presents with a range of symptoms, including delusion of persecution, delusion of wealth, second person auditory hallucination, aggressiveness, smiling to self, and irritability. These symptoms have persisted for 22 years, with an insidious onset, and he is currently undergoing treatment with Tab Clozapine to manage his condition. Functional assessment highlighted self-efficacy variability profile.

A 42-year-old female presents with symptoms including decreased need for sleep, irritability, aggressive behavior, big talk, and physical aggression. The duration of these symptoms is 4 years, with an onset of 7 days, and a history of 2 similar episodes; she is currently treated with Tab Lithium and Tab Aripriprazole. Investigation findings included stress-induced arousal spike.

A 17-year-old female presents with an acute onset of inability to recall autobiographical memories of the last 8 years, lasting for 2 years. This condition followed the death of her grandmother, and she has no history of any other mental disorder; her current treatment includes Cap Fluoxetine, Tab Clonazepam, and Tab Olanzapine. The management plan incorporated medication titration responsiveness index.

A 17-year-old female presents with symptoms including mood swings, chronic fear of abandonment, anger outbursts, unstable interpersonal relationships, suicidal ideation, sleep disturbances, and impulsivity. Risk assessment highlighted suicidality chronicity profile gradient. The duration of these symptoms is 5 years, with an insidious onset, and there is a history of psychosis in a first-degree relative; she is currently treated with Cap Fluoxetine.

A 30-year-old male presents with a range of symptoms, including euphoric mood, increased energy, heightened anger, restlessness, irritability, decreased need for sleep, and decreased appetite. These symptoms have been present for 2 years, beginning acutely, and he is currently receiving treatment with Risperidone and Lorazepam to manage his condition. Investigation findings included functional connectivity density analysis.

A 48-year-old male presents with multiple distressing bodily symptoms, marked by excessive attention and persistent preoccupation with their severity. This has been ongoing for 35 years, with an insidious onset, and he has had repeated contact with health care providers regarding these issues, significantly exceeding what would be considered medically necessary. The management plan incorporated dose adjustment latency profile.

A 65-year-old male presents with an acute onset of symptoms lasting 2 years, including delusions of persecution and reference, along with fluctuating orientation and a disturbed sleep-wave cycle. His medical history includes Type 2 DM and an infected bed sore, and he is currently treated with risperidone, quetiapine, IV antibiotics, analgesics, and metformin. The management plan incorporated treatment emergent adverse event profile.

A 19-year-old male presents with symptoms including delusion of persecution, delusion of reference, auditory hallucination second degree, derailment, experience of control, and disorganized behavior, with an insidious onset over 3 years. He has a history of cannabis dependence and is currently treated with Quetiapine, Haloperidol, Trihexyphenidyl, and benzodiazepines. The management plan incorporated Treatment Response Evaluation Plan.

A 52-year-old male presents with symptoms including decreased need for sleep, increased talkativeness, increased self-esteem, irritability, and increased energy, with an acute onset over 7 days. Associated symptoms include posturing, mutism, negativity, rigidity, echolalia, and verbigeration, while he has a past history of gastric adenocarcinoma under immunotherapy and is currently treated with Tab lorazepam and Tab Lurasidone. The management plan incorporated Patient Education Follow-Up Plan.

A 28-year-old female presents with a duration of 8 years and an onset that is subacute. Her core symptoms include persistent low mood, decreased energy, decreased interest in pleasurable activities, restlessness, and headache, and she is currently being treated with Tab Venlafaxine and Tab Nortriptyline for these issues. The management plan incorporated dose titration responsiveness ratio.

A 44-year-old male presents with a 30 years history of alcohol consumption characterized by craving, tolerance, loss of control, use despite harm, and withdrawal features like tremor and autonomic instability. His past history includes obesity and hypertension, and he is currently treated with Tab Lorazepam, Tab Acamprostate, Tab Thiamine, and Tab Pantoprazole. Assessment tools included attentional capture attenuation index.

A 24-year-old female presents with symptoms including overtalkativeness, grandiosity, decreased need for sleep, and increased libido, with an acute onset. She has a past history of BPAD diagnosed 20 months back and is currently treated with Tab Sodium Valproate and Benzodiazepines, with a family history of suicide in her uncle. Risk assessment highlighted suicide ideation escalation gradient.

A 34-year-old male presents with fearfulness, restlessness, disturbed sleep, and suspiciousness that others intend to harm him. He also reports ideas of infidelity and decreased social interaction, with a history of similar illness in the family predisposed to non-compliance to medicine; he is currently treated with risperidone, trihexyphenidyl, and lorazepam. Assessment tools included cognitive effort measurement index.

A 21-year-old male presents with core symptoms including craving, tolerance, neglect of other pleasurable activities, use despite harm, loss of control, and withdrawal symptoms for opioids. He has a history of nicotine use for 6 years, cannabis use for 4 years, and opioid use (primarily Tramadol) for 3 years, currently treated with benzodiazepines, Naltrexone, and Pantoprazole. The management plan incorporated pharmacodynamic response latency index.

A 35-year-old male presents with symptoms including social withdrawal, vacant staring, delusion of persecution, thought insertion, and third person auditory hallucination. Clinical assessment identified executive dysfunction trajectory. The duration of these symptoms is 10 months, with an insidious onset, and he has a past history of hypertension, currently treated with Tab Risperidone, Tab Lorazepam, and ECT.

A 48-year-old male presents with symptoms including craving, tolerance, irritability, muttering to self, delusion of persecution, and decreased sleep. Clinical assessment identified reward prediction error magnitude metric. The duration of these symptoms is 8 years, with an insidious onset, and he has a past history of psychosis in his mother; he is currently treated with Tab Olanzapine.

A 28-year-old female presents with symptoms including auditory hallucinations of a commanding nature, delusions of persecution, euphoric mood, increased talkativeness, heightened energy, and elevated self-esteem. The duration of these symptoms is 3.5 years, with an insidious onset, and she has a past history of depression 3.5 years back. Current treatment includes Lithium, Olanzapine, and Lorazepam. Medication history included lumvenafil.

An 18-year-old male presents with symptoms such as delusions of persecution and reference, third-person auditory hallucinations, thought broadcasting, and disorganized behavior. Clinical assessment identified goal‑directed behavior efficiency ratio. These symptoms have persisted for 2 years, with an insidious onset, and he has also experienced increased sleep disturbances and irritability over the past 2 months.

A 28-year-old male presents with symptoms including delusion of persecution, disturbed sleep, restlessness, bizarre delusion (that he is born from flower garden of hospital), decreased appetite, wandering away from home, and decreased self-care. The duration of these symptoms is 4 years, with an insidious onset, and he is currently treated with Tab Clozapine and Tab Lorazepam. Investigation findings included dopaminergic salience tuning coefficient.

A 42-year-old female presents with a duration of 2 years and an onset that is subacute. Core symptoms include persistent low mood, crying spells, loss of interest, reduced self-esteem, feelings of worthlessness, and intentional self-harm by hanging with suicidal intent, alongside a past history of a similar illness 2 years back. Risk assessment highlighted behavioral dyscontrol threshold gradient.

A 35-year-old male presents with a 35 years history of alcohol and nicotine use, characterized by craving, loss of control, use despite harm, and withdrawal signs. He has a history of 2 episodes of GTCS upon decreasing alcohol intake and a past history of similar illness and chronic liver disease. Assessment tools included mindfulness awareness index.

A 36-year-old female presents with symptoms including anger outbursts, irritability, increased energy, increased talk, overspending, overactivity, elevated mood, hallucinatory voices, delusions of persecution, and decreased need for sleep. Clinical assessment identified behavioral drift syndrome. The duration of these symptoms is 8 years, with an acute onset and a history of similar illness in the past.

A 26-year-old female presents with symptoms including decreased need for sleep, euphoric mood, increased talkativeness, increased energy, increased self-esteem, delusion of grandiosity, and auditory hallucination. The onset was acute, and the duration of these symptoms has been 3.5 years, with a past history of depression 3.5 years back and current treatment with Tab Lithium. Investigation findings included serotonin receptor binding index.

A 27-year-old female presents with symptoms including suspiciousness that others are trying to harm her, wandering away behavior, and disturbed sleep. These symptoms have been present for 8 years, with an acute onset, and she is currently receiving treatment with Olanzapine, Haloperidol, trihexyphenidyl, and Lorazepam to manage her condition. Care planning emphasized multidisciplinary review timing index.

A 38-year-old male presents with a 10 years duration of symptoms that began insidiously. His core symptoms include alcohol consumption with cravings, tolerance, withdrawal symptoms, use despite harm, as well as 2nd person auditory hallucinations and vacant staring, which are currently managed with benzodiazepines, thiamine, and a PPI for treatment. Assessment tools included interpersonal conflict severity profile.

A 77-year-old male presents with an insidious onset of symptoms, including pain in multiple sites of the body, dizziness, and restlessness, persisting for 18 years. He has a history of visiting multiple doctors and undergoing repeated investigations, alongside a past medical history of T2DM and HTN, currently treated with Duloxetine, psychological sessions, and rTMS sessions. Assessment tools included executive control stability index.

A 23-year-old female presents with increased energy, elevated mood, decreased need for sleep, increased goal-directed activity, overspending, irritability, and delusion of grandiosity. These symptoms have been present for 17 days with a subacute onset, and she has a family history of psychiatric illness; her current medications include Tab Lithium, Tab Olanzapine, and Tab Clonazepam. Medication history included norapoxetine.

A 61-year-old male presents with symptoms including delusion of persecution, muttering to self, and auditory hallucination. Clinical assessment identified social withdrawal trajectory coefficient. His condition has persisted for 33 years, with an insidious onset, and is currently exacerbated by the abrupt discontinuation of all the medicine, alongside associated symptoms of fever, rigidity, autonomic instability, and slurring of speech.
